title: korczewski as mentolder template (one component set, two themes)
description:
## Summary

- Retire `website/src/components/kore/` (13 files) — korczewski now renders the same mentolder component set, themed via existing `kore-website.css` + CSS-variable aliasing.
- `BrandConfig` extended with `navigation`, `footer`, `homepage.timeline`, `homepage.identityImage`. Both brand configs populated; `Navigation.svelte` + new `Footer.astro` consume them.
- `index.astro` collapsed to a single rendering path; `KoreLayout.astro` deleted; subpages (leistungen, [service], ueber-mich, referenzen, kontakt, impressum, datenschutz) wired to pass `brand="korczewski-kore"` to `Layout` so the kore theme actually applies.
- First-cut visual assets in `website/public/brand/korczewski/`: 7-icon SVG sprite (`icons.svg`) for service cards, placeholder `identity.svg` (K monogram), `og-card.png`. Designed to be swapped for commissioned art later.
- Adding a future brand now requires only `<brand>.ts` + `<brand>-website.css` + `<brand>-app.css`. No new components.

## Spec

`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-09-korczewski-as-mentolder-template-design.md`

## Known follow-ups

- `Hero.svelte` does not yet consume `homepage.identityImage` — korczewski still renders "PK" initials. Wire-up is a small follow-up.
- Brand-template procedure still wants a one-page README somewhere (mentioned in spec acceptance criteria #6).
- Two icons (`icon-ki-beratung`, `icon-deployment`) are the weakest of the seven — flagged for designer revisit.

## Test plan

- [x] `cd website && npm run build` passes
- [ ] `web.mentolder.de` visually unchanged after deploy
- [ ] `web.korczewski.de/` renders Hero → Stats → Services → WhyMe → Process → SlotWidget → FAQ → CTA → Timeline under kore theme
- [ ] `web.korczewski.de/{leistungen, ueber-mich, referenzen, kontakt}` and `/{service-slug}` render under kore theme
- [ ] Service card icons render via the SVG sprite (not blank squares)
